name: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc.
business: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. designs, manufactures, and provides zinc-based battery energy storage systems for utility-scale, microgrid, and commercial and industrial applications. The company also offers a battery management system, project management, commissioning, and long-term maintenance services.

exposures.sentence: Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. (NASDAQ: EOSE) ( Eos , we, us, our, or Company ) is an American energy company and America s leading innovator in designing, manufacturing and providing zinc-based battery energy storage systems ("BESS"), sourced and manufactured in the United States.

Eos Indensity can be deployed indoors or outdoors, including inside existing buildings. It addresses long duration, response driven use cases across data centers, military bases, manufacturing facilities and critical infrastructure.

These applications are increasingly relevant as overall electricity demand continues to rise, including incremental load growth associated with the expansion of artificial intelligence ("AI"), high performance computing ("HPC") and data center infrastructure, which is contributing to greater system capacity needs and grid level constraints.
We sell products directly to the electric utility industry and through sales channels to the commercial and industrial market.

The Company s primary applications include: (a) integration with renewable energy systems that are connected to the utility grid; (b) integration with renewable energy systems that are not grid connected;

Eos Indensity can be deployed indoors or outdoors, including inside existing buildings. It addresses long duration, response driven use cases across data centers, military bases, manufacturing facilities and critical infrastructure.

catalysts.event: Warrendale facility expected to become operational in 2026
catalysts.sentence: The Company also maintains a facility in Warrendale, Pennsylvania, which is not currently operational and is expected to become operational in 2026.

catalysts.event: Project AMAZE expanding manufacturing capacity to 8 GWh by 2027
catalysts.sentence: The DOE Loan Facility is a key step in advancing the Company's Project American Made Zinc Energy ("AMAZE") and is expected to fund the expansion of Eos manufacturing capacity to 8 GWh by 2027 to meet the growing demand for longer duration battery energy storage systems.

catalysts.event: Launch of DawnOS battery management software platform
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, the Company announced the launch of its new proprietary battery management system, software, controls and analytics platform, DawnOS, designed to revolutionize the way energy storage systems are managed, optimized and integrated into the grid.

catalysts.event: Supply agreement for up to 750 MWh with MN8 Energy
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, the Company announced a supply agreement for as much as 750 MWh with MN8 Energy, one of the largest independent renewable companies in the United States, to deploy Eos next-generation Z3 energy storage systems supporting a portfolio of projects that include providing clean, dispatchable power for large load applications.

catalysts.event: Memorandum of understanding with Frontier Power for 5 GWh framework agreement
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, the Company announced it has signed a memorandum of understanding with Frontier Power Ltd. ( Frontier ), a UK-based energy developer, for a 5 GWh energy storage framework agreement.

catalysts.event: Strategic collaboration with Talen Energy Corporation to develop energy storage capacity in Pennsylvania
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, the Company and Talen Energy Corporation announced a strategic collaboration to develop energy storage capacity across Pennsylvania to help meet the state s growing demand and support AI infrastructure.

catalysts.event: Order for $8 million BESS for Naval Base of San Diego
catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, the Company announced an $8 million standalone BESS order for the Naval Base of San Diego.

catalysts.event: Order with Faraday Microgrids for 3 MW / 15 MWh Z3 system on tribal land
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, the Company announced it has secured an order with Faraday Microgrids to deploy a 3 MW / 15 MWh Eos Z3 system for a commercial microgrid application on tribal land in California.

tamClaims.claim: BloombergNEF projects the global energy storage market will reach approximately 7,250 GWh by 2035.
tamClaims.sentence: As the Company continues to scale manufacturing to gigawatt hour ( GWh ) capacity, it expects to benefit from continued growth in the global energy storage market, which BloombergNEF projects will reach approximately 7,250 GWh by 2035.
